lines.text: Welcome back. It's {{CLOCK}}. We're {{ELAPSED}} into the night.
[pause] I want you to find a comfortable position. Sitting or lying down. Wherever you are right now — that is where you're supposed to be.
Close your eyes if you can. If you can't, that's fine. Just soften your gaze.
[pause] Breathe in through your nose. [pause] Four count. [pause] Hold.
Release through your mouth. [pause] Slow. [pause] Complete.
Again. In. [pause] Hold. [pause] Out.
[pause] You're safe where you are. The doors are locked. The windows are secure.
Or they're not. [pause] But you chose to stay. That choice is the safety.
Breathe in. [pause] The air tastes like smoke tonight. [pause] That's the fires. That's not your fire.
Hold. [pause] Release.
[pause] I want you to think about the people you decided not to call tonight.
Not because you don't love them. [pause] Because the line isn't safe.
Because your voice on their phone might draw attention. [pause] Breathe that thought out.
It's not abandonment. It's grace.
[pause] In. [pause] Four count. [pause] Hold.
The smoke is thicker now. Do you smell it? The visibility's dropped across the isthmus.
[pause] That smoke is carrying people's choices. [pause] We don't have to carry them.
Release. [pause] Slow.
[pause] Scan your body. Start at your feet. Are they tense?
Move up. Your legs. Your stomach. Your chest where your heart is working hard.
[pause] Your heart knows something you might not want to know. [pause] Let it know it anyway.
Breathe into that knowledge. [pause] In. [pause] Hold. [pause] Out.
[pause] The Order teaches that fear is just love with nowhere to go. [pause] You're loving your family right now.
By staying still. [pause] By staying quiet.
In. [pause] Four. [pause] Hold.
Release. [pause] Complete.
[pause] I want you to imagine a light. [pause] Not the sun. [pause] Something smaller.
A light in your chest. [pause] It's been there all along.
Tonight, while you stay still, it gets a little brighter. [pause] Does it?
[pause] Breathe toward it. In. [pause] Hold. [pause] Out.
[pause] There are people outside. [pause] You know that.
You can hear them sometimes. Or you're listening for them. [pause] Listening is its own kind of violence.
[pause] Stop listening. [pause] Breathe instead.
In. [pause] The smoke smells like oak and electrical fire. [pause] Someone's house just became a memory.
Hold. [pause] That memory is not yours to carry.
Out. [pause] Slow.
[pause] ka-lesh-mora-vein. [pause] Breathe.
In. [pause] Four. [pause] Hold.
[pause] When dawn comes — and it will — you will have made it through another night.
That is an accomplishment. [pause] That is a victory the world won't see.
Breathe into that. [pause] In. [pause] Hold. [pause] Out.
[pause] The clock reads {{CLOCK}}. [pause] We are {{ELAPSED}} in.
[pause] Somewhere on the East Side, the fires are still climbing. [pause] You are not responsible.
Somewhere on State Street, something is ending. [pause] You did not cause it.
Breathe. [pause] In. [pause] Four. [pause] Hold.
[pause] Out. [pause] Complete.
[pause] Rest now. [pause] Just rest.
We'll be here. [pause] The station will be here.
And you — you will be here, in the morning, having chosen silence.
[pause] That is enough.

tickerItems.text: Madison-wide cumulative: 61 confirmed (22:00 hour total +23). East Side showing 28% of night's kills so far.
Multiple structure fires reported across isthmus. Smoke reducing visibility to 3-4 miles. No official response available.
Capitol Square perimeter remains contested. Willy Street barricade holding steady. State Street ruins seeing coordinated activity.
"The ones outside are not your responsibility."
